context: =  extracellular deposits of insoluble fibrillar protein (most commonly amyloid AL = light chain) in myocardial interstitium Incidence:   AL amyloidosis (50%); AA amyloidosis (2%); isolated myocardial amyloid deposition (in 25% of autopsied patients > 85 years of age) Path:   commonly involvement of all 4 chambers CXR: √   normal / generalized cardiomegaly √   pulmonary congestion √   pulmonary deposits of amyloid NUC: √   striking uptake of 99mTc-pyrophosphate greater than bone (50-90%) ECHO: √   granular sparkling appearance of myocardium √   LV wall thickening √   decreased LV systolic + di
